channel.title: Virtualization Series on Anekoique's Blog
channel.description: Recent content in Virtualization Series on Anekoique's Blog

item.title: Virtualization Series
item.description: A guided tour of traditional machine virtualization, organized as ten chapters from foundations through performance, plus per-system case studies grounded in source-code reading.

item.title: Virtualization Systems — Xen
item.description: Canonical paravirtualizing Type-1 hypervisor. PV/HVM/PVH modes contrasted side by side across CPU, memory, I/O, cross-domain communication, and VM management. The disaggregated shape in production form.

item.title: Virtualization Series 00 — History
item.description: A historical preface to the survey, organized into five eras from IBM CP-40 (1964) through modern microVMs and language-isolated systems. Anchors each era in the papers that invented the techniques later chapters treat as ambient.

item.title: Virtualization Series 01 — Foundations
item.description: What virtualization is, why it is used, and the Popek–Goldberg condition for virtualizability. Sets up the trap-and-emulate discipline that the rest of the survey treats as ambient context.

item.title: Virtualization Series 02 — Taxonomy
item.description: The four largely-independent axes of VMM design: placement (Type 1 vs Type 2), guest interface (full vs paravirtual), hardware support (VT-x, EPT, IOMMU), and isolation boundary (hardware, software, language).

item.title: Virtualization Series 03 — Hypervisor Architecture
item.description: The recurring component set inside a VMM (vCPU, memory, device, interrupt/timer, exit handler, control plane) and the three architectural shapes (monolithic, hosted, disaggregated) they organize into.

item.title: Virtualization Series 04 — CPU Virtualization
item.description: How guest code actually runs on a physical CPU: trap-and-emulate, binary translation, paravirtualization, and hardware-assisted virtualization (VT-x, AMD-V). Plus vCPU scheduling, gang scheduling, and the exit-cost story.

item.title: Virtualization Series 05 — Memory Virtualization
item.description: From shadow page tables to nested paging (EPT, NPT), with the overcommit toolbox: demand allocation, ballooning, content-based page sharing, hypervisor swapping, idle-memory taxation.

item.title: Virtualization Series 06 — I/O Virtualization
item.description: Full device emulation vs paravirtual (virtio + vhost) vs direct assignment (SR-IOV). The order-of-magnitude performance spread, the role of IOMMU, APICv, and posted interrupts, and how all three coexist in production.

item.title: Virtualization Series 07 — Cross-Domain Communication
item.description: Hypercalls, microkernel IPC, shared-memory rings, grant tables, capabilities — the substrate every non-monolithic VMM is built on. Cost anatomy of boundary crossings and why disaggregation lives or dies by it.

item.title: Virtualization Series 08 — VM Management and Cloud Extensions
item.description: The operational layer: lifecycle, snapshotting, pre-copy / post-copy live migration, high-availability replication (Remus), microVMs (Firecracker), and fleet orchestration. How operational requirements shape VMM architecture.

item.title: Virtualization Series 09 — Performance and Overhead
item.description: What virtualization actually costs on modern hardware. CPU, memory, I/O, scheduling, and memory-pressure overhead components, and the residual costs that two decades of hardware extensions have not eliminated.
